cards.title: Dutch children's walking ritual promotes happiness and health
cards.body: Once yearly, Dutch kids, parents and teachers participate in a walking festival exploring neighbourhoods over four nights. This tradition appears genuinely transformative for exercise, friendship-building and wellbeing.

cards.title: Only children aren't lonely or maladjusted, says parent
cards.body: Persistent prejudices against only children and their parents ignore growing evidence of wellbeing. Single-child families are becoming increasingly common and thriving.

cards.title: Should kids climb neighbour's fence to retrieve ball?
cards.body: A parenting dilemma: does retrieving a ball over the fence teach trespassing or healthy independence? The debate highlights different parenting philosophies on risk and boundaries.

cards.title: Partner's cruel mother raises concerns for future children
cards.body: Advice columnist recommends minimising contact between a critical mother-in-law and potential future grandchildren unless she changes. Setting boundaries early protects children from harmful family dynamics.

cards.title: Momfluencers now co-parenting with AI assistants
cards.body: Some mothers are using AI to manage childcare and parenting tasks as gender imbalance continues in heterosexual households. This trend raises questions about technology's role in family life.

cards.title: How to stay safe swimming outdoors with children
cards.body: Experts recommend using designated swimming spots, understanding water hazards like riptides, and avoiding inflatables at beaches. Teaching water safety allows families to enjoy summer activities responsibly.

cards.title: Father's Day: importance of children feeling parental pride
cards.body: A child's certainty that parents are proud of them provides lasting emotional support. This feeling sustains children through discouragement and self-doubt throughout their lives.
